Sentence: At exam time, it is important to sleep well. Today we have Dr. Baker with us in the studio, and he is going to give us 5 top tips for getting a good night's sleep.
Welcome to the show, Dr. Baker.
Thank you. It's great to be here.
Don't go to bed with the television on.
Some people think they can sleep well with the TV on, but the noise and the lights mean you don't really sleep well.
Tip 2: Don't think too much before bedtime.
Do your hardest homework earlier in the evening. Do easier homework later. If your brain is too busy and full of ideas, it takes longer to get to sleep.
Tip 3: Don't play video games for an hour before you go to sleep.
They also make your brain too busy and active.
Tip 4: Turn off your cell phone when you go to bed.
What is so important that it can't wait until the morning? If possible, leave your phone in another room.
Tip 5: Play music if you like, But don't play it too loud.
Thank you, Doctor. That is very useful advice for our young listeners.
This recording was brought to you by the British Council.
